frozen: This file is committed before the PEP 770 survey corpus is downloaded. Categories, measurements, and falsification criteria may not change after results are observed. Amendments require a new versioned file with rationale; V1 results stand as reported under V1.

checker_scope: JSON parseability for JSON SBOM files
CycloneDX metadata.component.name compared with wheel metadata name when present
CycloneDX metadata.component.version compared with wheel metadata version when present
not_claimed: does not validate full CycloneDX or SPDX schemas
does not compare every SBOM component to every wheel file
does not trust, merge, or replace SPIRA evidence with embedded SBOM contents
does not perform vulnerability, license, malware, provenance, or legal analysis

corpus.source: PyPI packages shipping .dist-info/sboms/ per PEP 770, using public adoption data and/or a reproducible PyPI code index query recorded with the survey
corpus.selection: one wheel per package: latest x86_64 wheel when available, otherwise latest py3-none-any wheel when available
out_of_scope: other platforms for the same package
older versions for the same package

verdict_categories.SBOM_CONSISTENT: SPIRA's V1 PEP 770 subset evaluated the embedded SBOM and found no contradiction in the checked fields.
verdict_categories.SBOM_INCONSISTENT: SPIRA's V1 PEP 770 subset found a positive contradiction in the checked fields, such as CycloneDX metadata.component.name or metadata.component.version disagreeing with wheel metadata.
verdict_categories.SBOM_INVALID: An embedded JSON SBOM file could not be decoded as UTF-8 JSON or its JSON root was not an object. This is parseability invalidity only, not full schema invalidity.
verdict_categories.NOT_EVALUATED: An embedded SBOM is present but the relevant format or layer is outside SPIRA's V1 checker scope, such as non-JSON SBOM files or non-CycloneDX JSON. Explicitly reported; never counted as consistent.
verdict_categories.TOOL_ERROR: SPIRA crashed or produced unreadable output. This is a SPIRA bug or harness bug, never a finding about the package.

category_rules: Every wheel receives exactly one survey category.
TOOL_ERROR wheels are excluded from consistency statistics and reported separately with count and failure mode.
A wheel is SBOM_INCONSISTENT only on a positive contradiction in the fields SPIRA V1 checks.
Absence of checked data is never treated as inconsistency. Missing or unsupported evidence is NOT_EVALUATED, not SBOM_INCONSISTENT.
If a wheel contains multiple embedded SBOM files, the most severe supported result determines the wheel category: TOOL_ERROR, SBOM_INVALID, SBOM_INCONSISTENT, NOT_EVALUATED, SBOM_CONSISTENT.
SPIRA raw statuses are preserved in raw results; survey categories are a reporting layer over those raw statuses.

per_wheel: sbom_total_bytes: sum of embedded SBOM file sizes under .dist-info/sboms/
agent_summary_bytes: size of SPIRA agent_summary.json for this wheel
context_tax_measurements.baseline_definition: SBOM bytes only. Extended baselines, such as SBOM plus RECORD, may be reported separately only when clearly labeled.
not_claimed: one wheel per package; other platforms and versions are not measured

external_validation.known_external_observation: Prior public analysis reported 37 invalid embedded SBOMs in the surveyed ecosystem, attributed to one cargo-cyclonedx issue.
external_validation.rule: This observation is used as a comparison target, not as an automatic pass/fail gate, because SPIRA V1 does not validate full CycloneDX schemas. If SPIRA's SBOM_INVALID count differs materially, publication pauses until the discrepancy is explained as one of: outside V1 scope, survey corpus mismatch, SPIRA bug, harness bug, or external-analysis difference.

pilot.rule: Run a 10-wheel pilot before the full corpus. Any SPIRA crash or unreadable output is recorded as TOOL_ERROR and investigated before the full run.
pilot.reporting: Pilot results and any SPIRA bugs fixed during pilot are disclosed in the final report.

falsification_declared_in_advance: If most SBOMs are consistent under SPIRA's V1 subset, report that as an ecosystem-health finding.
If most SBOMs are small, for example below 3KB, and the median ratio is near 1, report that the PEP 770 context-tax claim is weak for today's measured corpus.
Any forward-looking claim that SBOM context cost may grow with automated auditwheel generation is labeled as expectation, not measurement.
If SPIRA cannot evaluate a large fraction of the corpus, report that prominently as a tool-coverage limitation.
If TOOL_ERROR is non-zero after pilot fixes, report it prominently as a SPIRA reliability limitation.
reporting_rules: Named packages with SBOM_INCONSISTENT or SBOM_INVALID findings are not published before contacting maintainers.
The final report must include not_claimed language for security, malware, vulnerability, legal, energy, and full schema validation claims.
All scripts, this file, raw results JSON, and generated aggregate tables are committed under research/pep770_survey/.
